genre_refs: John Ward Dunsmore, 'Washington and Lafayette at Valley Forge' (1907)
description: A straightforward reproduction of the historical oil painting 'Washington and Lafayette at Valley Forge' by John Ward Dunsmore (copyright 1907). It depicts George Washington and the Marquis de Lafayette on horseback in a snowy winter encampment, both in Continental Army uniforms (Washington with a buff-and-blue coat, Lafayette in a red-lined cloak), reviewing ragged, suffering soldiers huddled around a campfire among tents in the background under a cold orange-grey sky. The only on-image text is the printed lithograph caption strip along the bottom margin: 'M-6287. PAINTING BY DUNSMORE   WASHINGTON AND LAFAYETTE AT VALLEY FORGE   COPYRIGHT, 1907, BY BROWN & BIGELOW, ST. PAUL AND TORONTO'. This is a scanned/printed artwork rather than an agency-designed graphic; there is no slogan, seal, or overlay added by the Department of Labor. Used in a Christmas message invoking Valley Forge sacrifice and patriotism. Note: not a designed meme/poster (plain art reproduction with only its original caption).
notable_text: WASHINGTON AND LAFAYETTE AT VALLEY FORGE; PAINTING BY DUNSMORE; COPYRIGHT, 1907, BY BROWN & BIGELOW
